zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilson於1460年12月26日出生在英國維斯特莫藍德莫藍德時，她的父親James FitzPatrick20歲，母親Anne Jane Kelly20歲。她於1485年5月15日與Patrick Robert Wilson在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德莫藍德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1510年在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德莫藍德享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilson于1460年12月26日出生在英国維斯特莫藍德莫藍德时，她的父亲James FitzPatrick20岁，母亲Anne Jane Kelly20岁。她于1485年5月15日与Patrick Robert Wilson在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德莫藍德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1510年在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德莫藍德享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzPatrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Declan, Ronan, Liam, Seamus, Sinead, Siobhan, Aileen, Bridie, Brigid, Conan.

Irish: occasionally this may be a genuine Anglo-Norman French patronymic meaning ‘son of Patrick ’, but more often it has been adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Pádraig, ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Patrick’ (see Kilpatrick ).

variants.name: Patrick
meanings: Irish, Scottish, and English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French, Middle English, and Older Scots personal name Patrick (Old Irish Patraicc), derived from Latin Patricius ‘son of a noble father, member of the patrician class’. This was the name of a Christian saint, a 5th-century Romano-Briton who became the apostle and patron saint of Ireland, and it was largely as a result of his fame that the personal name was so popular from the Middle Ages onward. In Ireland the surname is usually Scottish in origin, from Scottish settlers in Ulster in the 17th century. See also Peden and McPadden , derived from pet forms of Old Irish Patraicc.
Scottish and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Scottish and Irish Gaelic Mac Phádraig ‘son of Patrick’.
English: variant of   Partridge  .

variants.name: Kilpatrick
meanings: Irish (Ulster): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phádraig ‘son of the servant of (Saint) Patrick’ (Irish Pádraig). See also Fitzpatrick .
Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called in Gaelic as cill Padraig ‘church of (Saint) Patrick’. See also Kirkpatrick .

variants.name: Fitchpatrick
meanings: Altered form of Irish   Fitzpatrick  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Grzegorz, Irena, Lucyna, Witold.

Polish (mainly Łada) and Czech (Moravian): from a short form of the Polish personal name Ładysław, Czech Ladislav, both from Vladislav (Latinized as Ladislaus), a Slavic name composed of the elements vlad ‘to rule’ (from Old Slavic vlasti, later vladati) + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’ (from slava ‘glory’). Compare 2 below.
Hungarian: from Lad(a), a short form of the personal name Ladomér, a Hungarian form of Slavic Vladimir (see Lado 2, compare 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Wilson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will + patronymic -son ‘son of Will’. Will was a very common medieval short form of William . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Willison
meanings: English: variant of   Wilson  .

variants.name: Will
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will, a short form of William . This surname is now commonly found in Scotland, in Aberdeenshire, Angus, and Kincardineshire.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or stream, from Middle English wel, wel(l)e, wille, in a southwestern dialect form. This surname is found predominantly in the south and southwestern parts of the country. Compare Well 1.
English: nickname from Middle English iwil, wil(l)e ‘agreeable, pleasing’.

variants.name: Willson
meanings: English (eastern): variant of   Wilson  .

nameMeanings.name: Emily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a medieval form of the Latin name Aemilia, the feminine version of the old Roman family name Aemilius (probably from aemulus ‘rival’). It was not common in the Middle Ages, but was revived in the 19th century and is extremely popular throughout the English-speaking world today. Its best-known 19th‐century bearer was probably the novelist and poet Emily Brontë ( 1818–48 ).

variants.name: Emilie
meanings: From Latin Aemilia. See Emily .

nameMeanings.name: Clara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Post-classical Latin name, from the feminine form of the adjective clarus ‘bright, famous’. In the modern English-speaking world it represents a re-Latinization of the regular English form Clare . It was made famous in the early 20th century by the singer Dame Clara Butt ( 1873–1936 ) and the silent film actress Clara Bow ( 1905–65 ), known as ‘the It girl’ (because, whatever ‘it’ was, she had it).

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilson was born on 26 December 1460, in Morland, Westmorland, England, her father, James FitzPatrick, was 20 and her mother, Anne Jane Kelly, was 20. She married Patrick Robert Wilson on 15 May 1485, in Morland, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1510, in Morland, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilson est née le 26 décembre 1460, à Morland, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, James FitzPatrick, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Anne Jane Kelly, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Patrick Robert Wilson le 15 mai 1485, à Morland, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1510, à Morland, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilsonが1460年12月26日にイギリスウエストモーランドモーランドで生まれた時，父James FitzPatrickは20歳，母Anne Jane Kellyは20歳であった。1485年5月15日にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドモーランドでPatrick Robert Wilsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1510年にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドモーランドで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilson(이)가 1460년 12월 26일 영국 웨스트모를랜드 모를랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James FitzPatrick(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Jane Kelly(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1485년 5월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 모를랜드에서 Patrick Robert Wilson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 모를랜드에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Emily Clara FitzPatrick - Lady Wilson родилась 26 декабря 1460 г. в местности морланд, вестморланд, Англия, её отцу по имени James FitzPatrick было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Jane Kelly, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Patrick Robert Wilson 15 мая 1485 г. в местности морланд, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1510 г. в местности морланд, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет.
